items.title: Mr Skelly animations tackling human themes of death, loss winning over fans worldwide
items.summary: An animation series tackling themes of grief, regret, love and gratitude has attracted an online fanbase of millions — and reflects the artist's search for peace and beauty.

items.title: Man charged with attempted murder, arson after house destroyed by fire at Clontarf
items.summary: Police will allege a 36-year-old man used accelerant to start a fire at a house in Clontarf yesterday.

items.title: Lights at Canberra's Manuka Oval lean inwards thanks to strict planning rule
items.summary: If the six light poles skirting Manuka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

items.title: Anniversary of national parks' return to traditional owners celebrated on NSW South Coast
items.summary: The Yuin people on the NSW South Coast celebrate two decades of Aboriginal ownership of two national parks.

items.title: Daria Kasatkina beaten by Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff's French Open defence ended by Anastasia Potapova

items.title: University students bioremediate Agent Orange chemicals manufactured for Vietnam War from Sydney waterways

items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal in a tense shootout in the Champions League final to be crowned European champions for the second straight year.

items.title: French Open men's draw back to glorious chaos with no clear favourite

items.title: Periods prove a big barrier for disabled women's participation in sport

items.title: Tasmania chosen to supply blood samples for global Parkinson's disease study
items.summary: Experts are predicting Parkinson's disease to double in the next 20 years, but little is known about what causes the condition and how to cure it. Tasmania has been chosen to contribute blood samples to a global study aimed at improving understanding of the genetics behind Parkinson's disease.

items.title: Colourism in the Pacific continues as region grapples with history of colonialism
items.summary: Skin bleaching and lightening products are widely sold in Tonga, which is part of a region with a long history of colourism.
